message:
Command `$app = @"
from flask import Flask
from models import SimpleModel

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def hello():
    model = SimpleModel('Test')
    return f'Hello {model.name}'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()
"@

$models = @"
class SimpleModel:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
"@

$reqs = @"
Flask
"@

New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path audit_task
Set-Content -Path audit_task\models.py -Value $models -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\my_rest_app.py -Value $app -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\requirements.txt -Value $reqs -Encoding UTF8

Get-ChildItem audit_task
Get-Content audit_task\my_rest_app.py` executed with exit code 0.

message.content:
Forge is treating this as a [api] based on your prompt. Generating plan...

<thought>
I need to create a directory named `audit_task` and populate it with three specific files:
1. `my_rest_app.py` containing a simple Flask application.
2. `models.py` containing a simple class definition.
3. `requirements.txt` containing the `Flask` dependency.

I will use PowerShell to create the directory and write the contents of these files using here-strings to ensure proper formatting and UTF-8 encoding. After creating them, I will verify their existence and contents.
</thought>

function.name: execute_bash
function.arguments:
{"command": "$app = @"
from flask import Flask
from models import SimpleModel

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def hello():
    model = SimpleModel('Test')
    return f'Hello {model.name}'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()
"@

$models = @"
class SimpleModel:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
"@

$reqs = @"
Flask
"@

New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path audit_task
Set-Content -Path audit_task\models.py -Value $models -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\my_rest_app.py -Value $app -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\requirements.txt -Value $reqs -Encoding UTF8

Get-ChildItem audit_task
Get-Content audit_task\my_rest_app.py"}

extras.command:
$app = @"
from flask import Flask
from models import SimpleModel

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/')
def hello():
    model = SimpleModel('Test')
    return f'Hello {model.name}'

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run()
"@

$models = @"
class SimpleModel:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
"@

$reqs = @"
Flask
"@

New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path audit_task
Set-Content -Path audit_task\models.py -Value $models -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\my_rest_app.py -Value $app -Encoding UTF8
Set-Content -Path audit_task\requirements.txt -Value $reqs -Encoding UTF8

Get-ChildItem audit_task
Get-Content audit_task\my_rest_app.py
